title: Identity, Authentication, Sessions, and Recovery · Decision Retrieval
summary: A delayed review reconstructs identity, authentication, sessions, and recovery from stakeholder, decision, assumption, revision, artifact, observation, changed case, rejected failure, repair, owner, and residual-risk evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for identity and session assurance model after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate person, account, organization, service, device, credential, authenticator, session, and display identity.
Choose authentication and step-up strength from threat, consequence, usability, accessibility, recovery, and platform evidence.
Define issuance, binding, rotation, idle and absolute expiry, revocation, concurrent sessions, device evidence, and secure transport.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-2 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-2
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Identity Entity · changed case · Review Identity Entity
steps.instruction:
review case — Account, person, credential, tenant, role, and session are conflated, and the accessible recovery path is weaker than ordinary sign-in. The review variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Separate person, account, organization, service, device, credential, authenticator, session, and display identity.

Which action demonstrates Identity Entity without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Identity Entity · changed case · Review Identity Entity.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate person, account, organization, service, device, credential, authenticator, session, and display identity. For Identity Entity · changed case · Review Identity Entity, keep Identity Entity connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Account, person, credential, tenant, role, and session are conflated, and the accessible recovery path is weaker than ordinary sign-in. The review variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Separate person, account, organization, service, device, credential, authenticator, session, and display identity.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Identity Entity condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: NIST Secure Software Development Framework — Controls prepared organizations, protected software, well-secured production, vulnerability response, provenance, verification, and release evidence.

hints: For Identity Entity · changed case · Review Identity Entity, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Identity Entity precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Identity Entity · changed case · Review Identity Entity, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: Account, person, credential, tenant, role, and session are conflated, and the accessible recovery path is weaker than ordinary sign-in. The review variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Separate person, account, organization, service, device, credential, authenticator, session, and display identity.

For Identity Entity · changed case · Review Identity Entity, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: One email address is a stable global person identity.

options.text: Separate person, account, organization, service, device, credential, authenticator, session, and display identity. Apply it to the stated input and retain a reproducible trace for identity and session assurance model.
One email address is a stable global person identity. That claim fails the identity entity · changed case evidence boundary for Identity Entity.

For identity entity · changed case, apply Identity Entity without its stated preconditions, omit the identity and session assurance model trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Identity Entity for identity and session assurance model.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Return usable authentication failures and telemetry without account enumeration, credential leakage, or inaccessible friction.
